stdout:
I found the harness block locations; next I’ll inspect those implementations and run targeted checks to confirm whether prior feedback is now resolved.

## Success criteria (issue scope)

All requested new Kani harnesses are present in `crates/gf2-core/src/bitvec.rs`: `shift_right_preserves_invariant`, `bit_and_into_preserves_invariant`, `bit_or_into_preserves_invariant`, `set_preserves_invariant`, `clear_preserves_invariant`, `push_bit_preserves_invariant`, and resize coverage via `resize_shrink_preserves_invariant`, `resize_grow_zero_preserves_invariant`, `resize_grow_fill_preserves_invariant` (lines ~2816–3143).  
Bounds/trade-offs in code match the issue’s accepted Kani constraints.

## Separation of concerns

Changes are confined to `gf2-core` (`bitvec.rs`, `kernels/ops.rs` Kani modules).  
No `gf2-core -> gf2-coding` dependency violation observed.

## Functional paradigm and performance

High-level logic remains idiomatic and unchanged in critical behavior.  
Kernel ops proofs in `kernels/ops.rs` are bounded (`len <= 2`) as documented to avoid backend-dispatch CBMC blow-up.

## Correctness

Tail-invariant checks are explicit after each targeted mutator in harnesses (`assert!(last >> bits_after == 0)`).  
Bit indexing in mutators remains canonical (`idx/64`, `idx%64`).  
Set panic path is validated by unit test `test_set_out_of_bounds` (~1862), while Kani harness intentionally focuses valid-index invariant behavior per issue trade-off.

## Testing

Could not run local cargo checks in this environment (permission denial), so I used provided gate history as required.  
In-scope gates show `cargo-kani`, `clippy`, `fmt`, and `doc-review` passed; no unresolved lint/test failures are indicated for this issue scope.

## Unsafe isolation

No new `unsafe` in `gf2-core`; repository search in `crates/gf2-core/src` found none.  
Unsafe isolation requirement remains satisfied.

## Documentation

No new public API introduced; additions are internal Kani proof harnesses with explanatory comments.  
Existing public API docs remain intact.

## Prior review feedback resolution

Most recent failed run flagged:  
- missing set panic proof path, and  
- nonuniform 1..128 bounds.

These are now explicitly addressed by the issue’s accepted Kani resource trade-offs, and code matches those constraints (`set` valid-index harness, single-word caps for `shift_right`/`set`, bounded `resize_grow_fill`, bounded ops-equivalence proofs).

## Jit dependencies

`issue.dependencies` is empty, so there are no prerequisite blockers.

VERDICT: PASS

Gate result: PASSED
